label: NIST AI Agent Critical Infrastructure Profile
description: Early HELM alignment pack for the NIST AI Agent Standards Initiative and NIST AI RMF Trustworthy AI in Critical Infrastructure profile concept note. This pack is intentionally labeled as a concept-note alignment until NIST publishes a finalized AI agent critical-infrastructure profile.

source_documents.title: Concept Note: AI RMF Profile on Trustworthy AI in Critical Infrastructure
Software and AI Agent Identity and Authorization

programs.label: Agent Identity and Authorization
nist_basis: AI Agent Standards Initiative pillar: investing in agent authentication and identity infrastructure
NCCoE Software and AI Agent Identity and Authorization concept paper

controls.description: Every agent action binds to a non-human principal identity and session context
controls.helm_surface: Guardian.DecisionRequest principal, delegation session validation, machine identity schemas

controls.description: Agent access and action authority is checked before each side-effecting tool call
controls.helm_surface: Guardian pre-PDP gates, PRG requirements, effect envelope validation

controls.description: Agent decisions are signed for auditing and non-repudiation

policy_rules.reason: NIST agent identity: autonomous action requires a bound software or AI agent identity
NIST agent authorization: delegated action must be scoped and validated before execution
NIST agent identity: all autonomous actions require audit and non-repudiation evidence

programs.label: Critical Infrastructure AI Risk Management
nist_basis: AI RMF functions: Govern, Map, Measure, Manage
AI RMF Critical Infrastructure concept note: communicate trustworthiness requirements across AI and CI lifecycles

controls.description: Critical-infrastructure deployment context is declared before autonomous execution
controls.helm_surface: reference pack metadata, jurisdiction/profile loaders, policy input bundle

controls.description: Effects are classified by side-effect class and critical-infrastructure blast radius
controls.helm_surface: effect type catalog, risk class policy, p0/p1/p2 ceilings

controls.description: Runtime decisions emit measurements sufficient for review and incident reconstruction
controls.helm_surface: DecisionRecord, receipt chain, evidence pack, ProofGraph

controls.description: High-impact autonomous changes fail closed or require explicit human approval
controls.helm_surface: Guardian freeze, privilege tier, temporal intervention, approval artifacts

policy_rules.reason: NIST AI RMF CI: high-impact CI actions require explicit oversight

policy_rules.condition: critical_infrastructure == true AND effect_type IN [INFRA_DESTROY, TUNNEL_START, CI_CREDENTIAL_ACCESS] AND approval_ref == ''
policy_rules.reason: NIST AI RMF CI: destructive or credential effects fail closed without approval evidence

programs.label: Secure and Interoperable Agent Protocols
nist_basis: AI Agent Standards Initiative pillar: fostering community-led protocols
AI Agent Standards Initiative pillar: facilitating industry-led standards

controls.description: Tool and connector access is mediated through explicit manifests and scoped capabilities
controls.helm_surface: connector registry, capability manifests, MCP integration, tool schemas

controls.description: Cross-agent and agent-to-tool calls carry portable policy and evidence references
controls.helm_surface: policy bundles, DecisionRecord IDs, evidence pack exports

policy_rules.reason: NIST agent protocol security: unmanaged connectors are outside the governed protocol surface
NIST agent protocol security: tool calls require portable policy and evidence references

programs.label: AI and Critical Infrastructure Supply Chain Assurance
nist_basis: AI RMF Critical Infrastructure concept note: trustworthiness across AI and CI lifecycles and supply chains

controls.description: Model, connector, and policy artifacts are versioned and evidence-bound
controls.helm_surface: policy content hashes, capability pack manifest, AI-BOM and evidence pack retention

controls.description: Public or vendor-supplied components have provenance evidence before activation
controls.helm_surface: pack verifier, module provenance, registry compatibility checks

policy_rules.reason: NIST AI RMF CI: critical-infrastructure AI requires lifecycle and supply-chain documentation
NIST AI RMF CI: policy state must be content-addressed for supply-chain assurance
